source_pr.title: sessionctx/variable: improve test coverage
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/35051

Problem Summary:

This is a function neutral change that moves around some tests (and a helper function). It improves the coverage of sessionctx/variable from 42.8% to 45.5% - but importantly the testing of the sysvar framework (variable.go) improves from 78.1% to 91.9%. Most of the lack of coverage is on sysvar.go - which is still at 31.4%. This is mostly because this code is indirectly tested in the other relevant packages.

### What is changed and how it works?

Test improvements.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: sessionctx/variable: improve test coverage.
